title: Will Donald J. Trump attend The 2026 FIFA World Cup Final?

current.lean_note: ADVERSARIAL VETO: The position is not supported by credible, specific evidence, and the divergence from the market implies a lack of alignment with broader consensus. The reasoning is internally inconsistent and lacks sufficient detail to justify the high co
adversarial_concerns: The proposed position (NO) contradicts the stated probability (YES) of 0.9148, creating internal inconsistency.
The market-implied probability (YES: 0.9595) is significantly higher than the proposed probability, suggesting the position is diverging from liquid market consensus without justification.
The reasoning cites 'official statements' from the White House but does not provide a specific, verifiable source or date, raising concerns about the credibility and timeliness of the evidence.
The forecaster's confidence is high despite the lack of concrete, publicly available evidence confirming Trump's attendance, indicating potential overconfidence.
current.rationale_summary: Multiple reputable and independent sources, including Reuters, BBC, The New York Times, and Fox News, confirm that the White House has officially stated that Donald Trump will attend the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final. Consistency across reports from varied outlets
reference_classes: Presidential attendance at major international sporting events

history.lean_note: I lean YES (my prob 85%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: Trump plans to attend (WH 'holding' his schedule); FIFA wants him to present the trophy; precedent at Club World Cup; marquee US-hosted event he is motivated to attend. Residual scheduling/security/health risk a month out.
reference_classes: stated-intention attendance at marquee events ~1 month out

adversarial_concerns: Divergence from liquid market implies unaccounted risk; forecaster assumes crowd is wrong without credible evidence
Residual 5% attributed to vague '26-day horizon' factors without quantified impact
Overconfidence in 'triple-confirmed' claims ignores potential for misinformation or misinterpretation of statements
history.rationale_summary: Triple-confirmed: Infantino stated day-of (Jun 23) he and Trump will jointly present the trophy; WH holding the July 19 date; Trump attended the 2025 Club World Cup final at the same MetLife venue and handed the trophy. NSSE security is an enabler; trivial NJ logistics. Residual ~5% is the 26-day horizon (US-Iran security, age 80).
reference_classes: president follow-through on stated easy-logistics appearance ~4wks out 92-97%

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 84%, modal YES) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: Multiple credible sources, including FOX 5 New York, The Tribune, The News Mill, and Al Jazeera, confirm or strongly suggest that Donald J. Trump will attend the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final, including reports that he will hand out the trophy at a match in New Jersey. Report of Trump handing out the trophy at a match in New Jersey

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 86%, modal YES) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: The base rate for a former U.S. president attending a non-U.S. international sporting event is low, especially one as high-profile as the FIFA World Cup Final. There is no direct evidence in the provided sources indicating Trump's attendance. While he may have a history of high-profile engagements, there is no clear indication of a specific event-related invitation or public commitment to attend.
key_drivers: Absence of confirmed invitation or public commitment
Low base rate for former U.S. presidents attending non-U.S. international sporting events
reference_classes: Former U.S. president attending international sporting events
High-profile individual attending major global events without public commitment
